title: Venator-class Star Destroyer
content:
{{Top|title=''Venator''-class Star Destroyer}} {{ShipSeries |image=[[File:Imperialattackcruisers.png]] |name=''Venator''-class Star Destroyer |designer=[[Lira Wessex]]{{BuildFalconCite|35|Starship Fact File}} |manufacturer=*[[Santhe Shipyards|Corellian shipyards]]''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]'' *[[Kuat Drive Yards]]''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' |line=''Venator''-class Star Destroyer{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode009.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Cloak of Darkness}} |type=*[[Battleship]]''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles]]'' *[[Cruiser]]{{TCW|Death Trap}} **[[Attack cruiser]]{{Databank|republic-attack-cruiser|Republic Attack Cruiser}} *[[Destroyer]]{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode011.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Dooku Captured}} **[[Star Destroyer]]{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode004.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Destroy ''Malevolence''}} |models=*[[Venator I-class Star Destroyer|''Venator I''-class Star Destroyer]]{{Armada|set=Venator-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack|cardname=''Venator I''-class Star Destroyer}} *[[Venator II-class Star Destroyer|''Venator II''-class Star Destroyer]]{{Armada|set=Venator-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack|cardname=''Venator II''-class Star Destroyer}} |availability= |role=Multi-role *Cargo [[transport]] *[[Command ship]] *[[Flagship]]{{Databank|imperial-star-destroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer}} *Flight school {{C|[[Imperial Era]]}} *Replenishment/supply vessel *Ship-to-ship [[cruiser]] {{C|initial and primary role}} *[[Starfighter]] [[carrier]] {{C|primary role}} *[[Troop carrier]] |firstuse=[[22 BBY]]''[[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition]]'' states that the Venator was first deployed during the [[Clone Wars]], which begins in [[22 BBY]] according to ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''. Venators also appear in the Clone Wars novel [[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']], which is also set in 22 BBY according to ''Timelines''. Therefore, the Venator must have been introduced in that [[Standard year|year]]. |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic]] **[[Republic Navy|Galactic Republic Navy]] *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems]]{{TCW|Point of No Return (The Clone Wars)}} **[[Confederacy of Independent Systems Navy]] *[[Galactic Empire]]{{Film|III}} **[[Imperial Navy]] }} {{Quote|Warning. Enemy capital ships. ''Venator''-class.|A [[Unidentified tactical droid|tactical droid]]|''[[Star Wars Battlefront II]]''}} The '''''Venator''-class Star Destroyer''', also known as the '''''Venator''-class cruiser''', was a class of more than 1,000 [[meter]]-long [[attack cruiser]] of the [[Star Destroyer]] design family that was operated by the [[Republic Navy|navy]] of the [[Galactic Republic]]. Designed by [[Lira Wessex]] to feature a double-wedge design and perform a wide range of missions, the Venator was the first class of Star Destroyer to be manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]]. Alternatively known as the '''Republic cruiser''', '''Republic attack cruiser''', or '''Republic Star Destroyer''', it was a versatile [[capital ship]] capable of serving as both a [[Warship|combatant]] in [[Starship|ship]]-to-ship [[Space warfare|naval warfare]] and a [[starfighter]] [[carrier]], while also being employed as a [[troop transport]], cargo ship, and supply and replenishment vessel. As a warship, Venators were protected by powerful [[deflector shield]]s and boasted a range of weaponry. For its carrier role, a large flight deck, along with [[hangar]]s to accommodate a complement of starfighters, [[gunship]]s, [[shuttle]]s, armored vehicles and other craft, was contained in the forward section, while three more docking bays were located around the ship. At least two variants existed, the [[Venator I-class Star Destroyer|''Venator I''-class]] and the [[Venator II-class Star Destroyer|''Venator II''-class]]. As the backbone of the Galactic Republic's [[Navy|navies]] during the [[Clone Wars]], the Venator was commonly employed by the [[Jedi General]]s of the [[Grand Army of the Republic]] as their [[flagship]]s, thus earning the moniker of '''Jedi cruiser'''. Deployed across [[the galaxy]] against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Venators saw action in many major engagements, such as the [[Battle of Christophsis]], the [[Malevolence Campaign|''Malevolence'' Campaign]], the [[Battle of Ryloth]], and the [[Second Battle of Geonosis]]. In the climactic [[Battle of Coruscant]] in the waning days of the war, over 1,000 Venators defended the [[Galaxy|galactic]] [[capital]] [[Coruscant]] against a massive Separatist [[Confederacy of Independent Systems Navy|fleet]]. Eventually, these starships emerged on the victors' side as icons of the conflict. When the Republic ended with [[Darth Sidious]]'s [[Great Jedi Purge|purge]] of the [[Jedi Order]] and his [[Proclamation of the New Order|establishment]] of its successor, the [[Galactic Empire]], the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers were absorbed into the nascent [[Imperial Navy]]. During the early [[Imperial Era]], Venators were utilized to consolidate Imperial authority, and later they fought against [[Separatist holdouts]] and fledgling [[Early rebellion against the Galactic Empire|rebel]] cells. They were also assigned to the private [[fleet]]s of [[Moff]]s, and to flight schools. Others were scrapped on [[planet]]s like [[Bracca]], where they were dismantled and salvaged by the [[Scrapper Guild]]. Although Venators were still active at the time of the [[Galactic Civil War]], they had mostly been replaced by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], a larger warship modeled on its Republic predecessor. ==Characteristics== ===General characteristics=== {{Quote|There's the Jedi cruiser.|A [[clone cadet]] of the [[Clone Youth Brigade]]|}} [[File:Venator_Databank.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Venator''-class was a class of Star Destroyer designed by Lira Wessex and manufactured by Kuat Drive Yards.]] The ''Venator''-class [[Star Destroyer]], or ''Venator''-class [[cruiser]],{{SW|url=series/the-bad-batch/battle-scars-episode-guide|text="Battle Scars" Episode Guide | The Bad Batch}} also called the [[Jedi]] cruiser,{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode013.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Jedi Crash}} Republic cruiser,{{TCW|Rising Malevolence|Rising ''Malevolence''}} Republic [[attack cruiser]],{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode002.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Rising ''Malevolence''}} or Republic Star Destroyer, was a type of cruiser-classed Star Destroyer. Beyond their status as cruisers, specifically attack cruisers, Venators were classified as [[battleship]]s and [[destroyer]]s. Designed by [[Lira Wessex]], it cost 59,000,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard|credits]]''[[Rise of the Separatists]]'' and was manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]] for the naval operations of the Galactic Republic in the [[Clone Wars]]. By the [[Imperial Era]], [[Santhe Shipyards|Corellian Shipyards]] were also involved in Venator construction. Spanning between 1,137''[[Star Wars: Absolutely Everything You Need to Know]]''''[[Star Wars Complete Vehicles, New Edition]]'' and 1,155.0 [[meter]]s in length, 548 meters in wingspan, and 268 meters in height, the Venator was defended by powerful [[deflector shield]]s and hull [[armor]] plating, while possessing an assortment of armaments. It was also equipped with a long-range [[sensor]] suite, as well as a [[navigation computer]] and corresponding systems. ''Venator''-class vessels generally carried a crew of 7,400 personnel, including [[officer]]s, [[pilot]]s and enlisted crew, but could also hold some 2,000 [[troop]]s in addition. 7,000 [[metric ton]]s of cargo could be transported aboard the Venator, as well as sufficient consumables to last two [[standard year]]s. In addition, ''Venator''-class vessels were equipped with safe rooms to be used by non-combat personnel/visitors in the event of an onboard emergency, so they would be safely out of the way and not impede crewmembers responding to said emergency. ===Hangar space=== [[File:VenatorHangar-OFNF.png|thumb|right|250px|An interior view of the main hangar of a Venator, facing aft]] {{Quote|Control failed to report in. We think they'll make their move to escape via the main hangar.''"
"''Affirmative, I want squads marshaled at position 2-5 and 2-7.|A clone trooper and [[Jesse]]|{{TCW|Victory and Death}}}} During the early period of the Clone Wars, the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer featured a wide array of [[hangar]] space. The forward section of the Star Destroyer featured a passage measuring 0.5 kilometers down the middle, with bays for [[starship]]s on either side. It was mainly accessed by large doors on top of the ship.[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] At the fore of this passage, at the bow the ship, was a collection of [[hyperspace transport ring]]s.{{TCW|Cargo of Doom}} Towards the aft of the passage, doors in the floor could open, leading to a space where [[Consular-class cruiser (Charger c70 retrofit)|Republic frigates]] could dock.{{TCW|Jedi Crash}} [[File:Venator-SWBC42.png|thumb|left|250px|A new version of the ''Venator''-class featured smaller doors on the ship's dorsal side.]] If no cruiser was docked, however, the space could be used by personnel to jump out of the Star Destroyer directly onto the battlefield,{{TCW|Gungan Attack}} or to deploy [[walker]]s without the need for [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/carrier|dropships]]. Overseeing the passage was a raised command station positioned at the aft. Even further aft, the ''Venator''-class had two independent docking bays on their port and starboard sides, again protected by [[blast door]]s. However, in later models, refits were made to incorporate more firepower at the expense of the main hangar. Towards the end of the war, a different type of Venator was used. These featured a set of smaller doors on the dorsal side of the ship, and lacked access from the ventral side altogether, with the blast doors that previously led to the main hangar space replaced with additional lighting, a crane and sometimes additional weaponry, such as a built-in [[beam weapon]]. Additionally, the side doors no longer led to independent hangars, but another passage that linked the two sides of the ship. The central hangar row then joined the smaller passage in the middle, replacing where the command station used to be. At the front of the main passage was a small opening [[starfighter]]s could launch from without having to go through any of the main doors. ===Bridges=== {{Quote|The dual bridge design of the ship, with one of the towers controlling starfighter operations and the other the ship's helm, allowed for a degree of contingency given the unpredictability of battle, but having them both within a single structure would have greatly improved efficiency and communication speed.|Historian [[Beaumont Kin]], on the pros and cons of the dual bridge design|''[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]''}} [[File:Venator_bridge.png|thumb|right|250px|The starboard bridge was designated as the command center, while the port bridge was for starfighter operations.]] ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers featured two [[Bridge (starship)|bridges]], with the starboard bridge serving as the primary command center of the ship and the port bridge being dedicated to starfighter operations. These bridges were raised from the main body of the destroyer and provided a wide, panoramic view of the battle. However, the bridges' exposed nature made them vulnerable to attack.{{TCW|Storm Over Ryloth}}{{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}} The bridges featured large windows at the front, with two sunken pits behind, with a raised command walkway in between. This design would become a staple in Imperial vessels, giving emphasis to the command hierarchy. Further behind, a [[holotable]] sat between two sets of blast doors which could be used for communication, ship analysis and troop movements. The dual bridge design allowed one portion of the crew to focus on starfighters while the other could control the helm when both bridges were operational. Furthermore, Venator's implementation of dual bridges provided the warship with a degree of contingency should one bridge be destroyed. However, the physical separation did end up disrupting efficiency and hampered speedy communication between members of the divided bridge crews. ===Weapons=== {{Quote|All batteries, open fire!|Jedi General Plo Koon|{{TCW|Destroy Malevolence|Destroy ''Malevolence''}}}} [[File:VenatorDBY827Turrets-CR.png|thumb|left|250px|Venators possessed multi-purpose DBY-827 heavy turbolaser cannons.]] As a capable warship, the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer boasted a myriad of weapons. On the dorsal side, eight double-barreled [[DBY-827 heavy turbolaser turret]]s, four on either side of the bridge area, were a key source of firepower. These heavy turbolasers had different modes of firing: one was to fire alternating between the two barrels, at variable but relatively rapid rates, while another was to fire both barrels simultaneously, but at a generally lower but still variable rate.{{TCW|Citadel Rescue}} The DBY-827's precise, long-range tracking was suited for hitting targets at a distance, while it could rotate in three seconds in the close-fighting, fast-tracking mode to engage targets nearby. DBY-827 turrets had seven different blast intensities so that the damage inflicted could be modulated under various circumstances, and the Venator could feed almost its entire reactor output to its heavy guns when needed. During the [[Battle of Bothawui]], one Venator had at least four DBY-827's on the ventral side of the vessel. Another form of firepower came in the [[AV-7 Anti-vehicle Artillery Cannon]], albeit mounted to the vessel without legs, which could be operated by a single soldier and was also used as an artillery piece for Republic ground forces. The Venator's artillery decks featured long rows of many such cannons that aimed approximately perpendicular to the length of the ship in order to fire at vessels across from it, usually at close range. The AV-7 cannons and their ammunition were susceptible to exploding if struck by enemy fire when Venators brawled with opposing ships. [[File:VenatorPointDefenseTurret-DT.png|thumb|right|250px|An anti-starfighter gun aboard the ''Endurance'']] The Venator was also equipped with at least fifty-two''[[Star Wars Journeys: Beginnings]]'' point-defense laser cannons for anti-starfighter purposes. At least one kind was a swiveling double-barrelled turret operated by a single gunner, which had a field of fire—left and right, and up and down—in which the turret could swing to attack a unit. For target practice, mechanical skeet packed with explosives could be launched from these gun emplacements, which a gunner would try to hit. Near the front of the ship, the ''Venator''-class had two sets of medium dual [[turbolaser]]s located along the hull trench, one on each side. At the aft, light laser cannons were used to defend against enemy starfighters. Four heavy [[proton torpedo launcher]]s and six heavy [[tractor beam projector]]s could additionally be found as part of the combat apparatus, as well as beam weapons attached to the ventral docking bay on certain Venators which were powerful enough to destroy a [[Munificent-class star frigate|''Munificent''-class star frigate]] with a concentrated shot. Such a blast could either come from an installed laser cannon or [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery]] Turbolaser.{{Armada|set=Venator-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack|cardname=SPHA-T}} Numerous other gun emplacements dotted the hull trenches of the Venator. ===Engines=== {{Quote|These chambers are quite the engineering marvel. This blast primer coating is capable of withstand—''"
"''No one cares! Keep moving.|[[Tech]] and [[Wrecker]], traversing inside a Venator's engine|{{TBB|Reunion}}}} [[File:CoburnsFlagship-CitadelRescue.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer featured ion thrust engines.]] The ''Venator''-class featured eight or ten [[Ion engine|ion thrust engines]] of three distinct sizes, with half on either side of the ship at the aft, arranged symmetrically. Each [[engine]] consisted of multiple chambers and rings, and the interiors were covered in a blast primer coating. The reaction that propelled the main engines consisted of a pulse combining to spark off the reactor core rods. If three engines on the starboard side and three on the port side failed, auxiliary power would activate. Additionally, the engines could be individually controlled from the command bridge. If an individual without protective gear was too close to the ion engines when they were online, they could suffer serious burns that would require immediate medical attention. However, these injuries could be recovered from if the victim's exposure to the ion thrust was minimal.{{TBB|Devil's Deal}} ===Landing capabilities=== {{Quote|Now our cruiser's ready to launch.|A clone trooper on [[Kashyyyk]]|}} [[File:Venator_takeoff.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Designated berthing docks for Venators on Coruscant]] A Venator usually required specialized landing strips to perform atmospheric landings, such as those at a [[Coruscant shipyard|cruiser staging area]] on [[Coruscant]], while the similar [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship|''Acclamator''-class assault ships]] could perform surface landings with their legs.{{TCW|Innocents of Ryloth}} However, surface landings on natural terrain could occur, as exemplified during the [[Battle of Kashyyyk]], when a Venator was able to touch down at the [[city]] of [[Kachirho]] and deploy Republic ground forces to reinforce the [[Wookiee]]s. The Venator also possessed retro rockets which could be used in atmosphere to correct uncontrollable entry. ===Complement=== {{Quote|Give me every fighter you can scrape up.|Admiral Wullf Yularen, to a clone navigation officer|{{TCW|Landing at Point Rain}}}} [[File:Btlbywing.png|thumb|left|250px|BTL-B Y-wing bombers launch from the ''Resolute''.]] Boasting an impressive starfighter complement, the Venator could carry various types of craft, including combinations of [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter]]s, [[Eta-2 Actis-class light interceptor]]s, [[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter]]s, as well as {{EncyclopediaCite|Republic, Rebel, and Resistance Starfighters}} [[V-19 Torrent starfighter]]s, [[BTL-B Y-wing starfighter/bomber]]s, and [[Clone Z-95 starfighter|Clone Z-95 Headhunters]], with some loadouts able to accommodate up to ninety-two starfighters of varying types. It could also hold up to forty [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry]]s, a variety of [[shuttle]]s, and [[landing craft]]. Venators could deploy some twenty-four [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]] walkers, other ground vehicles and modular components needed for a planetary [[garrison]].''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]'' ===Reactor and hyperdrive systems=== {{Quote|Over-fire all reactors. We're gonna blow right past this guy, whoever he is.|Jedi General Anakin Skywalker, facing down Admiral Trench|{{TCW|Cat and Mouse}}}} [[File:EnduranceEngineRoom-DT.png|thumb|right|250px|One such chamber aboard the ''Endurance'']] The main reactor of the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer was a large, dome-shaped chamber located along the center line of the ship, under the bridge tower. It could annihilate up to 40,000 tons of fuel per second at maximum power. Various reactant pumps and ducts, as well as cylindrical annihilation reactant silos, were located to the fore of the main reactor. At least one subsidiary reactor, also dome-shaped but significantly smaller, was found in the left aft "wing" of the vessel. This reactor was surrounded by a collection of power cells. At least one compartment pertaining to the reactor operations of the Venator contained three vertical shafts and walkways suspended over the chasm. Some walkways approached from three directions between the three shafts to meet at a control station in the middle. Clusters of cylindrical structures were located both below and above this station, as well as extensive tubing throughout the room. Damage to the different systems present in the chamber could sometimes leave the vessel crippled.{{TCW|Cloak of Darkness}} [[File:MaulTribunalHyperdrive-VaD.png|thumb|left|250px|Maul was able to destroy several hyperdrive components on the ''Tribunal''.]] Each ''Venator''-class ship was equipped with a class 1.0 main hyperdrive, as well as a class 15 backup hyperdrive. At least one hyperdrive generator was located at the back of the rearmost "fin" that separated the ion drive thrusters. In front of it was the main inertial compressor, with the stern power feeds slightly further to the fore. Similar to one of the reactor operations compartments found aboard Venators, the hyperdrive room was another cavernous chamber, with walkways on at least three levels intersecting above a deep chasm. Situated equidistant from a group of consoles, four tall columns spanned the height of the room, which could be damaged or even knocked completely loose by severe force. ==Role== {{Quote|Battle stations! Launch all fighters!|Jedi General Obi-Wan Kenobi|{{TCW|Bound for Rescue}}}} [[File:VenatorVentralView-SWE.png|thumb|right|250px|The ventral view of a Republic Venator]] As the [[flagship]] of the [[Republic Navy|Republic starfleet]], the ''Venator''-class was primarily honed for ship-to-ship combat. The large amount of dedicated space for carrying the starfighter complement also supported its duty as a combat ship. However, the Venator had other roles of being a cargo transport, troop carrier, and replenishment or supply vessel.{{SWE|technology|republiccruiser|Republic attack cruiser}} In the [[Imperial Military]], the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer was still notable as a capital ship that could enforce control over a situation.{{Databank|venator-class-star-destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer}} ==History== ===Clone Wars=== ====Early battles==== In [[22 BBY]], with the outbreak of the Clone Wars at the [[First Battle of Geonosis]],[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] the Venator was introduced to [[the galaxy]] as a vessel in the [[Republic Navy]]. A few weeks into the war, a Venator arose to the skies of the planet [[Coruscant]] and was seen by the newly-promoted [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] in the [[Emperor's office|Chancellor's office]], where he told [[Supreme Chancellor]] [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]] about his [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars|slaughter]] of the [[Tusken Raider]]s who kidnapped his mother [[Shmi Skywalker Lars|Shmi]]. Jedi General [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala]], and clone troopers of the [[7th Sky Corps]] were deployed from a ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer to the planet [[Krystar]] to [[Mission on Krystar|rescue]] Republic prisoners from Separatist captivity.[[Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales 3|''Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales'' 3]] [[File:BlockadeChristophsis.png|thumb|left|250px|The Galactic Republic deployed a fleet of ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers to break through the Separatist blockade of Christophsis.]] Early into the conflict, the Separatists [[Battle of Christophsis|invaded Christophsis]] to gain access to the [[Corellian Run]] and the resources on the planet surface.''[[Star Wars: On the Front Lines]]'' A [[Skywalker's fleet|fleet]] of three Venators, including the flagship ''[[Resolute]]'', and three [[Pelta-class frigate|''Pelta''-class frigates]] led by [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Admiral]] [[Wullf Yularen]] were sent to engage the thirty-ship [[Christophsis blockade|blockade]] under Admiral [[Trench]], but was driven to retreat behind the [[moon]] [[Leesis]]. The Republic force was bolstered by the arrival of Kenobi's Venator ''[[Negotiator]]'', carrying the prototype [[IPV-2C Stealth Corvette]], which Skywalker used to destroy Trench's ''[[Invincible]]'' and penetrate the [[blockade]]. While Skywalker delivered supplies to Senator [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]] on the surface, the rest of the Republic fleet attacked the blockade.{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode216.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Cat and Mouse}} During the ground war, the [[Jedi Council]] dispatched Yularen aboard a Venator to again penetrate the blockade and land [[Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano]] on the surface. On the ship's return journey away from the planet, it engaged three [[Munificent-class star frigate|''Munificent''-class star frigates]] and was able to withdraw. In the next attack, Yularen, accompanied by [[Jedi Master]] [[Yoda]], commanded the ''Resolute'' to lead two other Venators and four [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship|''Acclamator''-class assault ships]] to breach the Separatist blockade. The Republic forced the Separatist armada into retreat, allowing the Acclamators to reach the surface, deploy clone troops and secure a victory. Following the end of the [[Christophsis]] campaign, the [[501st Legion]] under Skywalker and Tano [[Battle of Teth|rescued]] [[Rotta]], [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]'s son, from the [[B'omarr Order Monastery]] on [[Teth]]. To reinforce the ground forces, Yularen arrived on a [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Teth)|Venator]], which deployed Kenobi and clone pilots aboard fighters before duelling a Separatist Munificent. A [[Vulture-class starfighter|vulture droid's]] suicide attack damaged the ship's port rear hangar, killing several clone pilots and crew, but the [[Mission to Jabba's Palace|mission]] to save Rotta culminated in a Republic success. [[File:TCW_Dao_fleet.png|thumb|right|250px|At least two Venators under Admiral Dao were lost at Ryloth.]] Later in the same [[Standard year|year]],''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the start of the [[Battle of Ryloth]], which is depicted in "[[Supply Lines]]," to [[22 BBY]]. the Confederacy [[Battle of Ryloth|invaded]] the [[Outer Rim Territories|Outer Rim]] world of [[Ryloth]], engaging at a [[Dao's fleet|fleet]] of least two ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers under Republic Admiral [[Dao]] which were protecting Jedi General [[Ima-Gun Di]] and the [[Outer Rim garrison]]. His fleet's necessary resources began to diminish, and, according to Master [[Mace Windu]], it was eventually destroyed. Relief supplies were illictly delivered to Ryloth's [[Twi'lek Resistance|pro-Republic resistance]] via neutral [[Toydaria]].{{TCW|Supply Lines}} Master Yoda then travelled to the moon [[Rugosa]] to negotiate plans for a Republic base on Toydaria with [[King]] [[Katuunko]], but was [[Battle of Rugosa|ambushed]] by the [[Dark Acolyte]] [[Asajj Ventress]] on [[Count]] [[Dooku]]'s orders. After Ventress's droid [[battalion]] was defeated and Katuunko's support won, Yoda's [[Consular-class cruiser (Charger c70 retrofit)|''Consular''-class cruiser]] returned with two Jedi cruisers. The Grand Master journeyed back to Coruscant aboard one of the Venators.{{StoryCite|story=Sharing the Same Face|book=The Clone Wars: Stories of Light and Dark}} ====''Malevolence'' Campaign==== {{Quote|And we have had no further contact with General Plo Koon. The absence of distress beacons indicates that his fleet was… that his fleet was destroyed, like the others. We are about to prepare a rescue mission.|Anakin Skywalker|audio=NoFurtherContactRescueMission.flac|''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' places the early events of the [[Battle of Ryloth]], in "[[Supply Lines]]," and the [[mission to Rodia]], in "[[Bombad Jedi]]," to [[22 BBY]]. As "[[Rising Malevolence|''Rising'' Malevolence]], which depicts the [[Malevolence Campaign|''Malevolence'' Campaign]], occurs between "Supply Lines" and "Bombad Jedi" according to {{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-clone-wars-chronological-episodeorder|text=''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' Chronological Episode Order}}, the ''Malevolence'' Campaign must have also occurred in 22 BBY. also saw Separatist [[Supreme Martial Commander of the Separatist Droid Armies|Supreme Commander]] [[Grievous]]'s multi–[[Standard month|month]] [[Malevolence Campaign|campaign]] to assault the Republic starfleet with the ''[[Malevolence]]'',{{EncyclopediaCite|Darth Maul and Other Followers of the Dark Side}} a [[Subjugator-class heavy cruiser|''Subjugator''-class heavy cruiser]] equipped with two [[mega-ion cannon]]s that could easily disable the systems of any ship. At the [[Battle of the Phu system]],{{BuildFalconCite|43|Starship Fact File}} Jedi Master [[Ares Nune]]'s{{BuildFalconCite|43|Starship Fact File}} {{C|German version}} [[task force]] of three Venators was destroyed by the ''Malevolence''. Grievous then proceeded to the [[Abregado system]], to which Master [[Plo Koon]] was dispatched to intercept the Subjugator. Nevertheless, the ''Malevolence'' [[Battle of Abregado|disabled]] the [[Plo Koon's fleet|fleet]] of three Jedi cruisers, including the flagship ''[[Triumphant]]'', with its port [[ion cannon]] and destroyed them all. While other Venators remained idle, Master [[Mace Windu]] announced a redeployment of all [[battle group]]s to convoy escort; the ''Negotiator'' was positioned in a defensive formation along with another Venator and Acclamators near a blue body in the [[Bith system]], contributing to the protection of the vital Republic convoys.{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode002.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Rising ''Malevolence''}} Skywalker and Tano embarked on an unsanctioned but successful mission to rescue Koon and discover the nature of Grievous's weapon. At the [[Battle of the Ryndellia system]], the ''Malevolence'' destroyed a medical convoy of one Venator and three Peltas, leading the Republic to predict that Grievous would next attack the [[Kaliida Shoals Medical Center]]. The [[Galactic Senate]] planned to send [[Shadow Squadron]], whose Y-wings were being housed aboard the ''Resolute'', to strike the enemy ship. The ''Negotiator'' and several other Venators were accompanying the ''Resolute'', and Kenobi decided to take the ''Negotiator'', ''Resolute'', and a third Venator on a longer route to the [[space station]]; Skywalker would lead his [[squadron]] through a shortcut in the [[Kaliida Nebula]] to intercept the Subjugator. After the Y-wings had incapacitated both ion cannons and saved the station, Kenobi's three Venators exited hyperspace and began a chase of the retreating Separatist vessel.{{TCW|Shadow of Malevolence|Shadow of ''Malevolence''}} Although the Venators' firepower could not destroy the ''Malevolence'', the massive ship was obliterated due to an erroneous hyperspace jump into the [[Dead Moon of Antar]], facilitated by a Jedi boarding action that sabotaged its navicomputer, thus ending the pursuit. ====Further Separatist plots==== {{Quote|The Republic fleet! We're outgunned! Get us out of here!|General Grievous orders his fleet to withdraw|{{TCW|Rookies (episode)}}}} [[File:Battle_over_Rishi_moon.png|thumb|right|250px|The fleet under Generals Kenobi and Skywalker struck against Grievous above the Rishi Moon.]] Sometime after the ''Malevolence'' Campaign, Republic starfighters under Skywalker [[Battle of Corvair|eliminated]] a Separatist fighter unit in the [[Corvair sector]]. In the aftermath, Yularen and Skywalker planned aboard the ''Resolute'' to neutralize a [[Kudo droid foundry|droid foundry]] on the moon [[Kudo III]]. Although the admiral wanted to strike the factory from afar, Skywalker elected to [[Mission to Kudo III|survey]] the location himself and discovered enslaved laborers there. Upon freeing the workers and returning to the ''Resolute'', Skywalker averted unnecessary bloodshed.[[Age of Republic - Anakin Skywalker 1|''Age of Republic - Anakin Skywalker'' 1]] Afterwards, a Republic fleet of at least three ''Consular''-class frigates and six ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers patrolled a quadrant of space in order to protect [[Kamino]] from enemy attack, particularly from the armada of General Grievous. [[Rishi Station]], a Republic outpost on the [[Rishi Moon]], was [[Defense of Rishi Station|attacked]] by Separatist [[BX-series droid commando|commando droids]], who hard-wired an all-clear signal to hide the approach of Grievous's fleet. The visiting [[clone trooper officer]]s and the surviving clones at the base eventually retook and destroyed the station, disabling the all-clear signal. Alerted, Skywalker and Kenobi moved their fleet to the [[Rishi system]] to face Grievous. Engaged by six ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers, Grievous believed that he was outgunned and ordered his force to retreat. Grievous initiated [[Skytop Station campaign|another offensive]], [[Battle of Falleen|defeating]] a [[Falleen battle group|Republic battle group]] at [[Falleen]] before proceeding to [[Bothawui]]. At the [[Battle of Bothawui|subsequent battle]], the ''Resolute'' and two other Venators under Skywalker and Yularen lured Grievous's six ''Munificent''-class vessels through the [[Fervse'dra asteroid belt]], where the Separatist fleet was completely destroyed by hidden AT-TEs and the Venators' gunfire; Grievous fled in his [[Soulless One|fighter]].{{TCW|Downfall of a Droid}} The campaign resulted in a Republic victory when Skywalker, Tano, and clone troops managed to [[Raid on Skytop Station|infiltrate and destroy]] the Separatist [[Skytop Station]] [[listening post]].{{TCW|Duel of the Droids}} ====Trouble with Nute Gunray and Dooku==== {{Quote|Those are Republic warships! Oh, the clones have arrived!|C-3PO rejoices the arrival of the ''Tranquility''|{{TCW|Bombad Jedi}}}} [[File:Tranquility.png|thumb|left|250px|The Jedi imprisoned Nute Gunray aboard the ''Tranquility''.]] When Senator [[Padmé Amidala]] traveled to [[Rodia]] for diplomatic talks with Senator [[Onaconda Farr]], she was detained by Separatist droid forces by order of [[Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray]]. Amidala's [[protocol droid]] [[C-3PO]] evaded capture and contacted a Republic clone trooper; the cruiser ''[[Tranquility]]'' was dispatched with the [[41st Elite Corps]], who captured Gunray after his battle droids had been defeated by the senatorial representative [[Jar Jar Binks]]. In [[21 BBY]],{{HelmetCollectionCite|30|Highlights of the Saga}} the Viceroy was taken aboard a ''Consular''-class frigate, accompanied by Jedi Master [[Luminara Unduli]] and Ahsoka Tano. He was transferred from the frigate to a prison cell on the ''Tranquility'', guarded by Captain [[Faro Argyus]], who was secretly a Separatist agent. The ''Tranquility'' was later attacked by [[Droch-class boarding ship|''Droch''-class boarding ships]], penetrating the Jedi cruiser's hull and deploying droids and [[Dark Acolyte]] [[Asajj Ventress]] onto the vessel. Ventress sabotaged the engine room, causing various malfunctions. She fought the two Jedi while Argyus escaped with Gunray. In one of the Republic's attempts to catch Count Dooku, General Skywalker was sent aboard [[Count Dooku's frigate]] under the pretense of surrender. General Kenobi later boarded the frigate and the two Jedi confronted Dooku. At the same time, the ''Resolute'' attacked the Munificent. Following an unsuccessful pursuit through the [[Vanqor system]] and on [[Vanqor]], the Jedi Generals were picked up by Tano and the clones, having been dispatched on a ''Nu''-class shuttle.{{TCW|Dooku Captured}} ====Outer Rim battles==== Above [[Quell]], [[Aayla Secura's task force]] of three Venators participated in a [[Battle of Quell|battle]] against at least five Munificents. With the situation desperate, the ''Resolute'' under Yularen and Skywalker was deployed to rescue [[Aayla Secura|Secura]] from her flagship ''[[Liberty (Venator-class)|Liberty]]''. Ultimately, all three Venators under Secura were destroyed,{{TCW|Jedi Crash}} and the Confederacy was victorious.{{Databank|super-battle-droid|Super Battle Droid}} During the battle, the Consular carrying Secura and her rescuers made an erroneous hyperspace jump to the [[Maridun system]]. On [[Maridun]], the Jedi [[Defense of Maridun|defended]] a [[Lurmen village]] from Separatist forces under weapons designer [[Lok Durd]]. Soon thereafter, the planet was secured by three of Yularen's Venators.{{TCW|Defenders of Peace}} [[File:Storm_Over_Ryloth.png|thumb|right|250px|The Republic was forced to retreat in the first attack.]] The Republic launched an offensive to retake Ryloth, sending a fleet of three ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers—the ''Resolute'', ''[[Defender]]'', and ''[[Redeemer]]''—under Skywalker, Yularen and Tano, to break Captain [[Mar Tuuk]]'s planetary blockade. While Skywalker and Yularen commanded the fleet from the ''Resolute'', Tano led [[Blue Squadron (Galactic Republic)|Blue Squadron]]. The Republic fleet was surprised by Separatist reinforcements consisting of four Munificents, prompting a withdrawal. Amid Tano's insubordination, a vulture droid rammed the ''Resolute''{{'s}} bridge, injuring the admiral. Although the ''Resolute'' and ''Defender'' were able to escape, the ''Redeemer'' was torn apart in space. After the ''Resolute'' and the ''Defender'' fled the system, Skywalker was contacted by Masters Windu and Kenobi, and was ordered to continue his attack unless the Republic's planned invasion was to be postponed. The ''Defender'' was badly damaged and in flames, and Skywalker created a strategy in which he alone would fly the Jedi cruiser into Tuuk's Lucrehulk. Tano, with the ''Resolute'', planned to use a [[Marg Sabl]] tactic to then attack the Munificents. The plan was a success; the ''Defender'' rammed the Lucrehulk, and the Y-wings from the ''Resolute'' disabled all six Munificents, allowing the [[Ryloth invasion fleet|Republic invasion fleet]] to land on Ryloth. The Republic's ground forces eventually retook the planet.{{TCW|Liberty on Ryloth}} ====Felucia and Devaron==== [[File:BattleOfDevaron-CoD.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Resolute'' destroyed Bane's fleet of Munificents.]] At the [[First Battle of Felucia]], [[Plo Koon's second fleet|Plo Koon's naval force]] of three Venators penetrated a Separatist [[Felucia blockade|blockade]] over the [[Felucia|planet]], destroying one Munificent and damaging another. Koon's fighters and gunships were then able to reach the surface and evacuate the stranded Republic troops. Later, [[bounty hunter]] [[Cad Bane]] [[Raid on the Jedi Temple|infiltrated]] the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] and stole a [[holocron]].{{TCW|Holocron Heist}} Bane wanted to open the device, so he led a Separatist [[Battle of Devaron|attack]] on Jedi Master [[Bolla Ropal]] on [[Devaron]]. The ''Resolute'' was deployed to the [[Devaron system]] to oppose Bane, destroying four Munificents and disabling the hyperdrive of [[Cad Bane's Munificent-class star frigate|Bane's frigate]]. Skywalker, Tano and the clones boarded the ship but failed to save Ropal, who was fatally electrocuted. In the ensuing skirmish, the Munificent was destroyed, and the Republic forces, along with Bane—disguised as a clone—returned to the ''Resolute''.{{TCW|Cargo of Doom}} Once aboard the Republic Venator, Bane stole a parked V-19 Torrent and Syliure-31 ring, escaping into hyperspace. Using the holocron's information, he kidnapped a [[Force-sensitive]] [[Zinn Toa|child]] on [[Mission to Glee Anselm|Glee Anselm]] and [[Wee Dunn|another]] on [[Second mission to Rodia|Rodia]] for Darth Sidious' experiments. On [[Naboo]], he was [[Attempted kidnapping of Roo-Roo Page|apprehended]] by the Jedi and transferred to a prison cell on the ''Resolute''. After his interrogation, the prisoner accompanied Windu and Kenobi to [[Black Stall Station]] where the Jedi [[Mission to Black Stall Station|recovered]] the holocron but Bane escaped. Having analyzed Bane's [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter|''Rogue''-class starfighter]] aboard the ''Resolute'', Skywalker and Tano [[Mission to Mustafar (Skywalker and Tano)|rescued]] the kidnapped children from [[Mustafar]].{{TCW|Children of the Force}} ====Carida incident==== {{Quote|The Jedi Council will be there, along with Republic officers from around the galaxy and… What the… The Separatists are going to ram the station with this ship!|Meebur Gascon|}} [[File:Renown-PoNR.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Renown'']] Later in the Clone Wars, the Galactic Republic had held a [[Republic strategy conference|strategy conference]] in the [[Carida system]], aboard the [[Valor (space station)|''Valor'']] orbiting the planet [[Carida]]. However, unknown to the Republic, the Confederacy of Independent Systems was planning to [[Carida incident|bomb]] ''Valor'' and end the conference. As part of their plan, the Separatist forces managed to hijack one of the Republic's ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers, the ''[[Renown]]'', which had been held in orbit above the Outer Rim world of [[Abafar]], a planet rich in an extremely valuable and highly volatile starship fuel known as [[rhydonium]], which the Separatists were mining from a [[Rhydonium mining installation|mining facility]] established on the surface by the Confederacy, with the intent of loading the Venator with thousands of canisters of rhydonium to transform the vessel into an unassuming bomb.{{TCW|Missing in Action}} Following their [[Escape from Abafar|escape]] from Abafar, the Republic's [[D-Squad]], a covert unit of Republic astromech droids and [[WAC-47|one]] [[DUM-series pit droid]], led by [[Zilkin]] [[Colonel]] [[Meebur Gascon]],{{TCW|Secret Weapons}} managed to board the Venator, assuming that the Republic had sent the ''Renown'' to rescue them. However, once they arrived on the Jedi cruiser's command bridge, they discovered that Separatist forces had hijacked the vessel, and, after narrowly escaping the pursuing battle droids, Colonel Gascon and the droids found a detonator painted in the respective colors of the Separatist Alliance in the center of the hangar bay control room, coming to the realization that the ship was truly a massive, unassuming bomb, opening the doors inside the hangar, revealing thousands of rhydonium canisters stacked together in each hangar, and upon sight of the canisters, Colonel Gascon remarked that the amount of rhydonium aboard the vessel could possibly destroy the entire Republic Navy. [[File:Carida_Incident.png|thumb|right|250px|A hijacked ''Renown'' was destroyed in a rhydonium-fueled explosion.]] While aboard the ''Renown'' in hyperspace, D-Squad came across a small group of Republic droids that served aboard the Venator before the Separatists took control, one of which was the [[LEP-series service droid]] [[BNI-393]], who informed the squad that the ship had been hijacked by the Separatists, and that its destination was a space station located within the Carida system, bringing the Zilkin to realize that the Separatists intended to use it to take out the Republic space station ''Valor'', the location of the Republic's strategy conference, forcing the team to take matters into their own hands and thwart the Separatists' plans by triggering the ship's explosion sooner than intended. However, the team soon ran into a series of complications as [[Unidentified super tactical droid (Carida)|the]] [[ST-series military strategic analysis and tactics droid|super tactical droid]] in charge of the operation sent a swarm of [[Pistoeka sabotage droid|buzz droids]] after the squad to dispatch them, but were eventually defeated at the cost of one of the team's members, [[M5-BZ]], who sacrificed himself in order to defeat the buzz droids by opening the airlock, pulling the buzz droids into hyperspace. After BZ's sacrifice, the ''Renown'' exited hyperspace in the Carida system, and the engines were put to maximum power. Before the super tactical droid could initiate the countdown sequence, he crossed paths with the astromech droid R2-D2, who managed to keep the Separatist droid out of reach of the detonator, granting him a small amount of time to trigger the detonation before it reached the station, resulting in a simultaneous explosion of the rhydonium canisters, destroying the Republic Venator and creating a large blast that engulfed the station and all vessels flanking it, sending large pieces of debris hurtling through space, obliterating a single [[Arquitens-class light cruiser|''Arquitens''-class light cruiser]], and damaging the station. ====Battle of Coruscant==== =====Caught by surprise===== {{Quote|The Battle of Coruscant toward the end of the war was one of the largest fleet battles in history, and the Venator proved to be a stable and flexible weapons platform in such an encounter.|Historian Beaumont Kin, ''[[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]|}} Nearing the end of the Clone Wars and, subsequently, the Galactic Republic, the Confederacy of Independent Systems—under the orders of [[Darth Sidious]], the true leader of the Confederacy—launched a massive and startling [[Battle of Coruscant|assault]] on the planet Coruscant in the [[Core Worlds]], the capital of the Republic, in an attempt to capture [[Supreme Chancellor]] Sheev Palpatine, who secretly orchestrated the battle and arranged for himself to be kidnapped by the [[Kaleesh]] [[cyborg]] Grievous as part of his plan to bring Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker closer to the dark side of the Force. Prior to the assault, the Republic and Separatist Alliance became enmeshed in the climax of the [[Outer Rim Sieges]] campaign, which was primarily fought on various worlds of the Outer Rim Territories, far from the Core Worlds and, consequently, Coruscant. As a result of the Sieges, most of the Republic's fleets, experienced officers and Jedi Generals were preoccupied on the various fronts of the campaign, leaving Coruscant sparsely protected by the Republic's remaining officers and the ill-prepared [[Coruscant Home Defense Fleet]], which had seen little to no action in the duration of the Clone Wars, giving the Separatists an opportunity to launch an unexpected and seemingly effortless attack on the Republic's capital.[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2016)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']] [[File:Battle_of_Coruscant.png|thumb|left|250px|Over a thousand Venators defended the Republic capital during the Battle of Coruscant.]] Despite the high belief that the Separatists would never attempt such an attack, the Confederacy unexpectedly attacked Coruscant with a fleet of over 1,000 starships led by Grievous' flagship, the modified [[Providence-class Dreadnought|''Providence''-class Dreadnought]] ''[[Invisible Hand]]'',{{Databank|general-grievous|General Grievous}} catching the Home Defense Fleet off guard and quickly overwhelming them, with the Chancellor, after a lengthy chase through the [[Federal District]], taken captive by Grievous back to the ''Invisible Hand''.''[[Star Wars Lightsabers: A Guide to Weapons of the Force]]'' Despite the initial surprise and overwhelming nature of the Separatist attack, the Republic was able to, in time, mount a defense. The Venator proved its worth throughout the battle as an effective weapons platform, performing its role with flexibility and stability. =====Reinforcements and victory===== {{Quote|Anakin, Rex, prepare all forces. We're jumping to hyperspace immediately.|Obi-Wan Kenobi|{{TCW|Old Friends Not Forgotten}}}} Learning of the Separatist attack, the Republic's Open Circle Fleet and Jedi Generals Kenobi and Skywalker were recalled to Coruscant to rescue the Chancellor from Grievous. The Coruscant Home Defense Fleet, despite being outnumbered, attempted to prevent the ''Invisible Hand'' from escaping with the Chancellor onboard. Before the Separatists could make their escape from the besieged capital, the Open Circle Armada and the two Jedi arrived in time, bolstering the number of ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers, including the Star Destroyer ''[[Ro-ti-Mundi]]'', to engage the large number of ''Munificent''-class star frigates, [[Recusant-class light destroyer|''Recusant''-class light destroyers]] and ''Lucrehulk''-class Battleships, which were escorting the ''Invisible Hand'' in order for it to flee, and allowed Generals Kenobi and Skywalker to enter the heart of the chaos of turbolaser fire and starships with their personal Eta-2 ''Actis''-class light interceptors. Struggling to reach the flagship due to the interference of hundreds of vulture droids, droid tri-fighters, and a brief evasion of [[discord missile]]s, two of which managed to deploy buzz droids onto Obi-Wan's starfighter, but were nonetheless destroyed. Returning to their flight path to the command ship, the Jedi quickly discovered that the hangar bay still had its deflector shields raised, forcing Anakin to open fire on a vulnerable point of the hangar to deactivate its shields, granting them mere seconds to enter the hangar before the blast shields were completely shut. [[File:Naval_style.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Guarlara'' sustained damage while trading fire with a Separatist warship, but emerged victorious after striking a crippling blow.]] While aboard the ''Invisible Hand'', the Jedi released the Chancellor from captivity and managed to kill the Sith Lord Count Dooku, the Confederate Head of State, and following their triumph over Dooku, the dreadnought briefly came into confrontation with the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer [[Guarlara (Venator-class)|''Guarlara'']] and the two starships exchanged turbolaser fire as they engaged one another, with ''Guarlara'' inflicting extensive damage upon the ''Invisible Hand'', causing it to momentarily plummet towards the planet below before the crew regained control. Despite rescuing the Chancellor and destroying a third of the CIS Navy, Grievous nonetheless managed to escape his flagship aboard an escape pod, which was retrieved by the ''Lucrehulk''-class Battleship ''[[Profusion]]''. Only days later, however, Kenobi killed Grievous and, shortly after that, Skywalker turned to the [[dark side of the Force]]. He [[Mission to Mustafar|slaughtered]] the [[Executive Separatist Council|Separatist Council]] on [[Mustafar]] and ordered the Trade Federation to deactivate all remaining Separatist droids immediately, officially ending the Clone Wars. ===Siege of Mandalore and Order 66=== {{Quote|This ship is going down, and those soldiers, my brothers, are willing to ''die'' and take you and me along with them!|Rex to Ahsoka Tano|}} [[File:TribunalTrailingSmoke-VaD.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Tribunal'' was lost along with its crew during the onset of Order 66.]] The ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer ''[[Tribunal]]''''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Character Encyclopedia - Join the Battle!]]'' of the Open Circle Fleet was commanded by Ahsoka Tano and her special [[332nd Division|division]] of the 501st Legion. The Jedi cruiser ferried Tano, Commander Rex and the division back to Coruscant following the successful capture of former Sith Lord [[Maul]] on Mandalore, with Maul a restrained and guarded prisoner in the detention level. En route to Coruscant, however, [[Order 66]] was implemented and the clones [[Skirmish aboard the Tribunal|turned]] on Tano, also attempting to kill Maul on Darth Sidious' orders only for him to be freed by Tano as a distraction. This allowed her to ambush Rex and have his [[Behavioral modification biochip|control chip]] removed, freeing him from the brainwashing. The ensuing skirmish aboard the Republic Venator resulted in it crashing onto a desolate [[Unidentified moon|moon]] after Maul destroyed the hyperdrive, with the loss of all hands aboard save for Maul, Tano and Rex. The wreckage of the Venator would remain on the moon well into the [[Imperial Era]], becoming buried in snow, until it was discovered by the Empire and investigated personally by Darth Vader, Skywalker's Sith identity, who retrieved his former apprentice's [[Ahsoka Tano's lightsabers|lightsaber]] from the crash site before departing.{{TCW|Shattered}} Another [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Zeffo)|Venator]], commanded by Jedi Master [[Chiata]], engaged in [[Battle against a Separatist dreadnought|battle]] with a Separatist dreadnought in the final hours of the Clone Wars. Though it managed to destroy the opposing warship, the Jedi cruiser was itself critically damaged and ended up crashing on the isolated world of [[Zeffo]]. Chiata, her Padawan [[Marseph]], her unit's [[Unidentified Clone Commander (Zeffo)|Clone Commander]] and a majority of the [[Chiata's unit|clone unit]] survived the crash, but the [[Skirmish on Zeffo|implementation]] of Order 66 saw the deaths of all involved, with Chiata being buried in the cruiser's wreckage by her Padawan before he succumbed to his own wounds and the clones being buried by the native villagers, who set up a shrine to them near the crash site. The [[Crash Site|wreckage]] of this Venator would also endure into the time of the Empire, becoming inhabited by various native fauna. [[File:Albedo-Brave-destroyed.png|thumb|right|250px|The ''Albedo Brave'' was destroyed by Jaro Tapal after his clone troopers turned against him.]] The ''[[Albedo Brave]]'' was a ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer commanded by Jedi Master [[Jaro Tapal]] and his [[13th Battalion|13th "Iron" Battalion]]. The ''Albedo Brave'' was stationed in orbit above [[Bracca]], having successfully secured the planet after a lengthy [[Bracca Invasion|campaign]], and was due to move on to [[Mygeeto]] when Order 66 was issued, forcing Master Tapal and his Padawan [[Cal Kestis]] to [[Skirmish aboard the Albedo Brave|fight for their lives]] against their formerly loyal comrades. Though the two Jedi managed to kill many of the clones in their path, including the battalion's [[Unidentified Clone Commander (13th Battalion)|commander]], and reach the escape pod bay, they were outnumbered and Tapal was fatally wounded just as they boarded an escape pod. The ''Albedo Brave'' was subsequently destroyed due to Tapal sabotaging its reactors to mask their escape, taking all the surviving clones aboard with it. ===Imperial service=== ====A new era==== {{Quote|Though the Venator served the Republic's purposes well during the Clone Wars, there were definite limitations to its design and image that made it unsuitable for the Empire. ''[…]'' Furthermore, its official name was not nearly foreboding enough for Imperial purposes, not to mention the ship had popularly been referred to as the "Jedi cruiser" during the conflict. Given the nature of Order 66 and the Great Jedi Purge, the Empire had no wish to continue utilizing tools associated with a hated enemy.|Beaumont Kin, ''The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire|}} [[File:VenatorStarDestroyerDatabank-RtK.png|thumb|left|300px|''Venator''-class Star Destroyers were inducted into the Galactic Empire.]] In the aftermath of the [[Proclamation of the New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers were still in service as a capital ship of the [[Imperial Navy]]. Imperial Venators were repainted to remove the red markings of the Republic, becoming whitish-grey in color like their successor warships.{{TBB|War-Mantle}} In the Imperial Starfleet, the Venator stood out for its ability to dominate, and [[Imperial officer]]s of the new government were given leadership over these Star Destroyers. Nonetheless, the Empire was eager to adopt a more foreboding capital ship and factor in the lessons learned from the Clone Wars with the Venator. In particular, the Venator had become tied to the image of the Jedi Order during the war as the "Jedi cruiser," something the Empire wanted its new ship to move away from instantly. A [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Death Star)|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] newly constructed by Santhe Shipyards was the transport of newly-christened [[Galactic Emperor]] Sheev Palpatine shortly after the transformation of the Republic into the Empire for an inspection visit{{InsiderCite|226|A Certain Point of View}} to the under-construction [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|DS-1 Death Star Mobile Battle Station]] in orbit of Geonosis. Joining the Emperor aboard the destroyer was Wilhuff Tarkin, who was granted the title of [[Moff]] under the new regime, and Anakin Skywalker, who had adopted the new identity of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth]] Vader upon his fall to the [[dark side of the Force]] at the end of the war. Due to the high-ranking personnel aboard, the Venator was free to jump from the Core Worlds to Geonosis without needing to first check in with [[Sentinel Base]]. Joined in space by at least two other Venators, the vessel was assigned to protect the Death Star.{{ForceCollection|Barrow Oicunn|star=3}} ====Darth Vader's flagship==== {{Quote|Droid, prepare my meditation chamber.|Darth Vader, to the MSE-6-series repair droid|{{StoryCite|story=No Good Deed...|book=Darth Vader (2017) 1}}}} [[File:Vaders_Venator.png|thumb|right|250px|Darth Vader was in charge of a ''Venator'' shortly after the Empire was formed.]] One [[Darth Vader's Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] operated under the command of Vader and carried his personal [[Darth Vader's meditation chamber|meditation chamber]] aboard. While preparing to meditate in his chamber, Vader was interrupted by Admiral [[Mummert]], who told him that he could not deploy Imperial starships to the [[Hortolo system]] by the set deadline. The Sith Lord, irritated by the admiral's ineptitude, choked him to death by way of the Force. Next, Vader was stopped by an [[Unidentified Imperial general (Winnikk system)|Imperial general]], who reported Imperial troop deployments to the [[Winnikk system]]. Because Vader had ordered 50,000 troops to be dispatched, and only 10,000 were, the dissatisfied Sith Lord choked the general to death with the Force as well. A third Imperial officer disturbed Darth Vader, announcing that his personal TIE fighter could not be repaired, despite the efforts of the crew. Once again tired of the excuses, Lord Vader used the Force to choke him to death too. Before beginning his meditation, Vader became irritated at the [[MSE-6 series repair droid|MSE-6-series repair droid]] in the room, which had observed the whole episode, because it bumped into his leg, and destroyed it. By [[14 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' this Venator had been replaced by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Perilous]]'' as Darth Vader's flagship.''[[Lords of the Sith]]'' ====Skirmish on Bracca==== In the weeks following the rise of the Galactic Empire, a ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer from the very first production line was among numerous other older models awaiting to be scrapped on Bracca. It was visited by Captain Rex and Clone Force 99 in order to use its onboard medical facilities, similar to those on the Tribunal, to remove the latter's control chips. After a brief skirmish where [[Wrecker]]'s control chip was activated and caused him to attack his comrades, the chips were successfully removed and Rex departed from Bracca whilst the Bad Batch remained behind to salvage munitions from the Venator's weapons bay. However, they were spotted by members of the local Scrapper Guild and their presence reported to the Empire, which dispatched their former comrade Crosshair and a complement of Imperial troops to eliminate them. Despite the rather deteriorated condition of the wreck, many of the Venator's systems remained operational including engines and weapons and the Bad Batch made use of both to escape the Imperial troops. As they were attempting to escape through the Venator's main starboard engine, Crosshair ordered the engine turned on in order to kill them but the Bad Batch managed to escape this by detonating salvaged explosives within the engine tube, causing it to break off whilst the ion drive was still active. Crosshair was exposed to the full power of the ion drive and sustained severe burns to his face before it was switched off, necessitating immediate medical attention. ====Razing Tipoca City==== {{Quote|Sir, we're in position.''"
"''Open fire.|A clone crewmember and Vice Admiral Edmon Rampart|{{TBB|Return to Kamino}}}} [[File:ImperialVenatorsOverKamino-RtK.png|thumb|left|250px|A fleet of Imperial Venators was in orbit of Kamino during the evacuation.]] Three ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers of the Imperial Navy were stationed above [[Tipoca City]] during the [[Attack on Kamino|operation]] to withdraw the Empire's clones from Kamino in the early months of the regime. Goods and personnel were flown out from the city on various transport craft to the Imperial Venators, with [[Vice Admiral]] [[Edmon Rampart]] and [[Commander]] [[Crosshair]] supervising the process. Later, as the evacuation was nearing completion, the Venators had moved further away from the city. Crosshair brought his former squadmate, the renegade [[clone commando]] [[Hunter]], who had been captured on a [[Extraction on Daro|rescue mission]] on [[Daro]], to Tipoca City, intending to draw the rest of [[Clone Force 99]] into a [[Rescue of Hunter|trap]]. However, after the Bad Batch had arrived, Crosshair killed his [[Elite Squad|squad]] to appeal to his brothers to join him. [[ES-02]], realizing this betrayal, left the city aboard the last outgoing ship, returning to the Venators. On the bridge of one of the Venators, Vice Admiral Rampart informed [[Governor]] Wilhuff Tarkin that all personnel and technology were safe in Imperial control. Tarkin then commanded him to destroy Tipoca City. The Imperial Venators proceeded forwards through the stormy clouds before pausing once the ships were in the correct position. Upon Rampart giving the order to clone navigation officers to open fire, the three warships unleashed a steady barrage from their starboard and port dual medium turbolaser cannons. Setting the city alight with fiery explosions, the turbolaser fire ripped through the domed buildings, toppled over platforms, and felled the underwater stilts, plunging the burning facilities into the depths of the ocean. [[File:VenatorsDestroyTipocaCity-RtK.png|thumb|right|250px|Edmon Rampart's three Venators bombarded Tipoca City to ruins.]] The aerial bombardment persisted, with the [[Tipoca City Military Complex]] knocked into the water when the Bad Batch, Crosshair and [[AZI-345211896246498721347|AZI-3]] were still inside. After the guns had fallen silent, a [[Unidentified clone trooper (VZ-114)|clone stormtrooper]] reported to Rampart that all Kaminoan facilities had collapsed into the sea. Satisfied with the outcome, the Vice Admiral withdrew his ships to rendezvous with the fleet, leaving the remains of the city to sink beneath the waves. In this way, the Empire destroyed Tipoca City, although the Bad Batch and their companions ultimately managed to escape their underwater predicament.{{TBB|Kamino Lost}} ====Stationed across the galaxy==== Several [[Standard month|months]] after the destruction of the Kaminoan cities and cloning facilities, a Venator was deployed to the planet of [[Desix]] after the planet had been [[Mission to Desix|conquered]] for the Empire by a [[Crosshair and Cody's team|unit]] of clone stormtroopers led by Clone Marshall Commander [[Cody]]. The Venator deployed more [[Imperial Military|Imperial forces]] to occupy the planet and also received goods that had been looted from the planet's farmers. In [[18 BBY]], over a year after the formation of the Empire, multiple ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers were stationed on Coruscant, with several remaining in orbit above [[Imperial City]]. Among the ships was Vice Admiral Rampart's personal Venator, [[VZ-114]], which was docked at [[Coruscant shipyard|Coruscant's Imperial Shipyard]] for maintenance and repairs. As his Venator was worked on, Rampart attended the [[Imperial Senate]] to oversee the passing of the [[Imperial Defense Recruitment Bill]], which would see the formalization of the [[Imperial Military]]'s transition from a clone army to a recruitment-based force. In order to expose Rampart's treachery to the Senate, Senator [[Riyo Chuchi]] worked with the rogue clones of Clone Force 99. As Chuchi stalled the passing of the bill in the [[Imperial Senate Building|Senate Building]], the rogue clones snuck aboard Rampart's Venator in order to obtain its command log, which held the records of Kamino's destruction. After battling through the ship's clone stormtrooper crew and extracting the command log, the clone mercenaries fired the Imperial Venator's engines and escaped on an escape pod as VZ-114 slid off of its [[docking clamp]]s and collapsed to the bottom of the shipyard. Ultimately, the command log was delivered to the Senate Building, where recorded footage of Rampart's fleet destroying Kamino was shown to the entirety of the Senate. Rampart was subsequently arrested, but the Imperial Defense Recruitment Bill was still passed on the behest of Emperor Palpatine.{{TBB|Truth and Consequences}} ====Cloned Zillo Beast incident==== Later that same year, a juvenile [[Zillo Beast]] cloned by the Imperial [[Advanced Science Division]] escaped from captivity, and caused the [[Transport 904|vessel]] it was aboard to crash on the planet [[Silla]]. As a result, two Imperial Venators were dispatched to the planet to recapture the beast, who had grown substantially after feeding upon electricity. After dropping out of hyperspace, one of the Venators deployed numerous [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter|Alpha-3 ''Nimbus''-class V-wing starfighters]] and [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry|LAAT/I gunships]], which descended upon the planet's surface and engaged the Zillo Beast clone, who was eventually recaptured.{{TBB|Metamorphosis (episode)}} ====Skirmishes with rogue clones==== In 18 BBY[[The Bad Batch Season 2|''The Bad Batch'' Season 2]] episodes up to "[[Truth and Consequences]]" can be placed in [[19 BBY]] per ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''. The twelfth episode, "[[The Outpost]]," establishes that the [[Galactic Empire]] sent [[Mayday's squad]] to [[Barton IV]] over a [[Standard year|year]] prior, establishing that over a year has passed since the [[Proclamation of the New Order|beginning]] of the [[Imperial Era]]. This places the episode around [[18 BBY]] per ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''. Therefore, episodes "[[Spoils of War]]" though "Truth and Consequences" take place in 19 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]], "[[The Crossing]]" through "[[Metamorphosis (episode)|Metamorphosis]]" take place between 19 BBY and c. 18 BBY, and "The Outpost" onwards are c. 18 BBY. a Venator came to the aid of a [[Unidentified Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser (Balmorra)|''Gozanti''-class cruiser]] under the command of [[Captain]] [[Pearce]], which was under attack by rogue clones.{{TBB|Tipping Point}} Soon after, several Imperial Venators were deployed to the planet of [[Ord Mantell]] as Imperial forces conducted a raid in [[Ord Mantell City]] to capture the rogue clone "Omega", while also subsequently occupying the planet.{{TBB|Plan 99 (episode)}} Many months later, a Venator attacked [[Pabu island]] on the planet of [[Pabu]], where Omega was hiding after escaping Imperial custody. The Venator deployed numerous [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry|LAAT/is]] to the island's surface, where stormtroopers and clone commandos rooted out Omega after a [[Raid on Pabu|brief raid]]. Soon after, two Imperial Venators accompanied [[Wilhuff Tarkin]]'s personal ''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer to the planet of [[Wayland]] to secure [[Tantiss Base]] after a clone prisoner uprising and Zillo Beast containment breach had nearly destroyed the facility. After it was deemed that the base was unsalvageable, any remaining equipment and research was flown out of the base and into the Imperial Star Destroyer and the two Venators to be taken off-world. ====Construction of the Death Star==== [[File:Moff_Tarkin.png|thumb|left|250px|Darth Sidious, Darth Vader, and Wilhuff Tarkin observed the construction of the Death Star from the bridge of a ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer.]] While the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|DS-1 Death Star Mobile Battle Station]] was being built in the orbit of Geonosis, at least three Venators were posted near the gargantuan [[battle station]]. On board one [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Death Star)|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]], both Darth Sidious, the [[Galactic Emperor]], and Darth Vader watched the construction of the [[superweapon]], accompanied by Tarkin. The bridge crew of this Venator was no longer comprised of clone naval officers, but non-clone human personnel, such as [[Barrow Oicunn]], who had previously served in the Republic Navy but was now devoted to the Empire.''[[Star Wars: Force Collection]]'' {{C|Card: Barrow Oicunn}} ====Campaign at Salient==== During the [[Salient Campaign|campaign at Salient]] in the [[Salient system]] in [[18 BBY]], [[Moff]] Wilhuff Tarkin commanded multiple ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers reassigned from [[Telos]]. Supporting Tarkin's ''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer ''[[Executrix]]'', the Venators launched ARC-170 starfighters as part of the operation against [[Captain]] [[Saw Gerrera]]. At this time, both the Venator and the ARC-170 were considered older-generation models by Tarkin, which were starting to be phased out.''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]'' ====The capture of Thrawn==== In [[15 BBY]], the crew of a Venator dubbed the ''Strikefast'' encountered an [[Unidentified planet (Thrawn's exile)|Unidentified planet]] while pursuing smugglers in [[Wild Space]]. Following Imperial [[Unknown Alien protocols|UA Protocols]], teams of clone stormtroopers and [[Imperial Navy Trooper]]s were deployed on the planet's surface in an effort to identify any [[Sentience|intelligent]] [[alien]] life. Nearby, an exiled [[officer]] of the [[Chiss Ascendancy]], [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], took notice of the Imperial presence and continually harassed the troopers as they conducted their search. Eventually, Thrawn managed to steal the [[Phase II clone trooper armor|armor]] off of a clone stormtrooper that he had killed, and snuck aboard the ''Strikefast,'' though he was quickly discovered. Impressed with the Chiss' immense knowledge in strategy and tactics, [[Captain]] [[Voss Parck]] brought Thrawn back to Coruscant for a personal audience with the Emperor.[[Thrawn 1|''Thrawn'' 1]] ====Further Imperial service==== Sometime between [[9 BBY]] and [[2 BBY]],The events of "[[The Path of Anger]]" take place during [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo|Thrawn's]] [[time]] as an [[Galactic Empire|Imperial]] [[admiral]], which corresponds to between [[9 BBY]] and [[2 BBY]] per ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''. at least four ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers served as escorts to an ''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer above Coruscant where [[Morgan Elsbeth]] was presenting her designs of the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter]] to the Empire.{{TOTE|The Path of Anger}} An Imperial Venator was present above an Imperial [[Cassaro shipyard|shipyard]] on [[Cassaro]], where it provided security for the installation as Imperial Star Destroyers were constructed within.{{TOTU|Freinds}} ====Pacification of Namzor==== Around [[2 BBY]], a ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer under the command of Imperial [[Moff]] [[Ahr]] arrived above the planet of [[Namzor]] in order to crush an [[Namzor insurgents|insurgent uprising]] and secure the rebels' [[Coaxium]] supplies. Numerous V-wing starfighters were deployed from the Imperial Venator and proceeded to strafe the insurgents positions, inflicting heavy casualties. Noticing a deposit of Coaxium near the insurgent lines, Ahr recalled the starfighters back to his Venator so that the supplies could be secured. Darth Vader however, who had been participating in the attack, ignored Ahr's orders and continued strafing the rebel positions until both the insurgents and the Coaxium deposit were destroyed.[[Age of Rebellion - Darth Vader 1|''Age of Rebellion - Darth Vader'' 1]] ====Open War==== At least one ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer would go on to [[Battle between the Rebel Alliance and Empire|fight]] on the frontlines of the Galactic Civil War together with half a dozen ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers.{{FFGXW2|set=Core Set|cardname=Predator}} ===Legacy=== ====Scrap yards across the galaxy==== [[File:VenatorShipbreaking-JFO.png|thumb|right|250px|The Galactic Empire scrapped many older ''Venators'', especially as the larger ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer came into production.]] As early as [[19 BBY]],"[[Battle Scars]]" takes place shortly after the [[Proclamation of the New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[19 BBY]]. many older ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers from the first production line and other Republic Era starships were beginning to be scrapped on planets like Bracca, which was the site of a major campaign during the war. The breakdown of many of these older Venators lasted into 14 BBY.''[[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]'' The salvaged parts were meticulously picked through by large numbers of workers and the salvageable material was used to support the construction of newer Imperial machinery and ships.''[[The Art of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]'' ====Wreckage and use==== The design of the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer ultimately evolved into the similarly wedge-shaped [[Victory-class Star Destroyer|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] and ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer, the signature [[battleship]] of the Galactic Empire.{{SWE|technology|imperialstardestroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer}} However, it was still in production by [[5 BBY]] and at least one ''Venator''-class was stationed at the [[Anthan Prime Orbital Dockyard]] several [[Standard month|months]] after the [[Battle of Yavin]].[[Darth Vader (2015) 9|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 9]] During the [[Battle at Padmé's tomb]] around [[3 ABY]],The events of ''Darth Vader'' 4 take place shortly after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. Since ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'' to [[3 ABY]], the events of ''Darth Vader'' 4 must also take place around that [[Standard year|year]]. the former [[Naboo Royal Handmaidens]] of [[Padmé Amidala]] understood they would be unable to best the Sith Lord Darth Vader, but [[Eirtaé]] used the opportunity to strike, and therefore tap into, [[Darth Vader's armor|Vader's armor]] with her electrified [[spear]]. Given an incomplete mess of code, Eirtaé traveled to the wreck of a Venator that had seen use in the Republic Era and tapped into its systems, cross referencing Vader's data with its technology to find forty-seven percent of Vader's command codes.[[Darth Vader (2020) 30|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 30]] [[File:Venator-Zavian-Abyss.png|thumb|left|250px|left|Vanguard Squadron happened upon the wreckage of a Venator in the Zavian Abyss.]] A nearly-entirely intact [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Level 201)|Venator]] was left abandoned on [[Level 201]] of Coruscant, with the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'' racing past the forsaken Star Destroyer during the [[Assault on Coruscant|aerial assault on Coruscant]].[[Star Wars (2020) 36|''Star Wars'' (2020) 36]] During the construction of the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|second Death Star]], ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers still remained in active service to the Empire, where several were used to protect the [[Sanctuary Pipeline]] leading to the [[Endor system]] and the Death Star II. Following the Death Star II's [[Battle of Endor|destruction]] in [[4 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the main events of ''[[Star Wars: Squadrons]]'' to [[4 ABY]]. the [[New Republic]]'s [[Vanguard Squadron]] came across the [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Zavian Abyss)|wreckage]] of a ''Venator''-class, while escorting three [[GR-75 medium transport]]s, before coming into contact with the ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer [[Overseer (Imperial-class)|''Overseer'']]. This was part of a New Republic [[Ambush at the Zavian Abyss|ambush]] in the [[Zavian Abyss]], where they revealed their prototype ''[[Starhawk]]''.''[[Star Wars: Squadrons]]'' Over fifty years after the Clone Wars,''[[Resistance Reborn]]'' the wreckage of ''Venator''-class Star Destroyers still existed throughout the galaxy.{{FFGXW2|set=Hotshots and Aces II Reinforcements Pack|cardname=Zay Versio}} The wreckage of a Venator was located on the junk world of the [[Necropolis]] by the time of the [[New Republic Era]].[[Age of Resistance - Rey 1|''Age of Resistance - Rey'' 1]] In [[34 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' the [[Force-sensitive]] [[scavenger]] [[Rey Skywalker|Rey]] visited the Necropolis in order to repair the ''Millennium Falcon''. She was granted permission to salvage spare parts by [[Ara-Nea]], a [[Junk boss|proprietor]] of the Necropolis, who charged the scavenger five hundred credits. Surveying the junk field from the dorsal side of a Venator, Rey considered it a "feast" as opposed to a typical [[Standard day|day]] of scavenging for her on the planet [[Jakku]], where she had lived before entering the struggle between the [[Resistance]] and [[First Order]]. Around that same time, decommissioned Venators still littered the world of Bracca and were flown past by a [[T-70 X-wing starfighter]] flown by a [[Resistance]] operative who journeyed to Bracca as part of a team in search of starships for the [[Resistance navy]]. ==Behind the scenes== ===Development=== [[File:Venator-class_Cross-section.png|thumb|right|250px|Cross-sections of the ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer.]] The ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer first appeared in the film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. In Latin, the word ''vēnātor'' means "hunter."{{WP|Venator}} The opening hangar doors across the middle of the Venator were seen in ''Revenge of the Sith'' concept art by [[Ryan Church]]{{Twitter|rchurchdesign|status/1684393114092568577|[[Ryan Church]]|quote=Here's my first approved rough sketch and a detail/color pass I did over ILM Modeler John Goodson's maquette for this ship- you can see that Lucas shortened the tail of this ship between the sketch and the maquette-|archivedate=20230729153509}} but would not be seen in action until{{Twitter|pabl0hidalgo|status/1684416830662901762|[[Pablo Hidalgo]]|quote=Note the full open hangar doors along the middle - which didn't get fully realized until The Clone Wars animated series.|archivedate=20230729153216}} the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' micro-series{{CW|23}} and the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' series. The dual bridges of the Venator helped suggest the craft was more of a warship: in Church's mind, the Venator was given an extra bridge after losses sustained to previous craft.{{Twitter|rchurchdesign|status/1684584051926237186|[[Ryan Church]]|quote=2/2- the dual bridge for this sketch was to suggest more of a warship with a redundant bridge (Doug Chiang's Ep2 Cruiser wasn't a wartime-designed ship, I figured that this was and was changed/improved based on hard-won lessons from operating the earlier design)|archivedate=20230729154410}} Church saw the Venator as a ship designed during the Clone Wars, rather than before it, and hoped the design made it clear to audiences that it was not the same vessel as the ''Acclamator''-class seen in [[Star Wars: Episode II
